## Takeaways

- Earlier positional encodings add position to the token representation before attention.
- RoPE puts position inside attention by rotating $Q$ and $K$.
- Values are not rotated; they are still blended by the attention weights.
- The query-key dot product depends on relative distance between positions.
- Modern GPT-style models often prefer RoPE because it gives attention a strong relative-position signal without adding learned position parameters.

::: {.callout-note}
*What's next?*
RoPE changes how one attention head sees position.
The next chapter asks how a model can track several kinds of relationships at once: *multi-head attention*.
See @sec-ch08-multi-head-attention.
:::
